content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(6, 7, 1), (4, 8, 4), (5, 9, 5), (1, 4, 9), (2, 4, 11), (4, 9, 13), (0, 9, 17), (4, 5, 17), (0, 2, 18), (1, 5, 19), (2, 6, 21), (0, 4, 24), (2, 5, 26), (6, 8, 29)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 2 exactly at time 21.

First, we will go through each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (6, 7, 1) has node 6 and time 1. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (4, 8, 4) has node 4 and time 4. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (5, 9, 5) has node 5 and time 5. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (1, 4, 9) has node 1 and time 9. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 4, 11) has node 2 and time 11. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (4, 9, 13) has node 4 and time 13. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (0, 9, 17) has node 0 and time 17. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (4, 5, 17) has node 4 and time 17. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (0, 2, 18) has node 0 and time 18. -> Add node 2.
* Edge (1, 5, 19) has node 1 and time 19. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (2, 6, 21) has node 2 and time 21. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (0, 4, 24) has node 0 and time 24. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 5, 26) has node 2 and time 26. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (6, 8, 29) has node 6 and time 29. -> Add node 8.

Now we have the following nodes linked with node 2 at time 21:
[4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 15, 16, 19, 20, 24, 26]

Answer: [4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 15, 16, 19, 20, 24, 26]
